analysis:
Here's the market intelligence brief:

---

**IRAN CONFLICT — MARKET INTELLIGENCE BRIEF | 28 MAR 2026**

• **Markets price near-zero chance of March resolution, pivot to April diplomacy.** With ceasefire-by-March at 3% and military action through March 31 at 95%, traders have fully written off any near-term halt. The April 30 ceasefire contract at 40% ($6.2M volume — the deepest liquidity bucket) is now the key focal point, reflecting cautious optimism around the Trump administration's 15-point peace plan and the 10-day pause on energy strikes extended through April 6.

• **Iran's rejection of the US ceasefire plan is the dominant market driver this week.** Tehran called the 15-point proposal "maximalist and unreasonable" on March 25 and issued its own 5-condition counterproposal demanding reparations, sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and security guarantees. Markets responded by keeping April 7 ceasefire odds pinned at just 13%, signaling traders see no breakthrough before counterproposal negotiations even begin.

• **The term structure gradient (13% → 23% → 40% → 54% → 62% → 76%) implies a grinding negotiation, not a sharp resolution.** Each monthly bucket adds roughly 10-15pp, consistent with a slow diplomatic process mediated through Pakistan and other intermediaries. The 83% year-end probability on the broader "conflict ends" market suggests strong consensus this is a bounded conflict — but one measured in months, not weeks.

• **Escalation risk remains live despite the diplomatic track.** On March 27, 10 US service members were injured in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, and US-Israeli strikes in western Iran killed at least 20 civilians. The 11% odds on a US ground invasion by March 31 and the 34% probability of Iranian regime collapse before 2027 reflect tail risks the market is actively pricing.

• **Cross-market signal: the "conflict ends" market trades 5pp below the "ceasefire" market at the April 30 tenor (35% vs 40%).** This spread implies traders believe a ceasefire could precede a formal conflict resolution — consistent with Trump envoy Witkoff's statement that direct negotiations could begin "this week" and Israel's separate escalation in Lebanon (1,142 killed since March 2) complicating any comprehensive deal.

• **Outlook: Watch the April 6 energy strike pause expiration as the next binary catalyst.** If Trump extends the pause again or Witkoff secures direct talks, expect the April 30 ceasefire contract to break above 50%. A resumption of energy strikes would likely compress the entire curve back toward single digits through April 15. The $35.7M volume on the March 31 ceasefire contract — despite 3% odds — signals heavy speculative interest and potential for violent repricing on any surprise.

---

Sources:
- [Iran calls US proposal 'maximalist, unreasonable' — Al Jazeera](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/25/iran-calls-us-proposal-to-end-war-maximalist-unreasonable)
- [Trump extends pause on Iran energy strikes — NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/03/26/nx-s1-5761882/iran-war-peace-conditions)
- [Iran rejects US ceasefire plan, issues counterproposal — PBS](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/iran-dismisses-u-s-ceasefire-plan-and-issues-its-own-counterproposal)
- [Day 28: US service members injured at Saudi base — CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/27/world/live-news/iran-war-us-israel-trump)
- [Iran rejects Trump's proposal, lays out 5 conditions — NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/03/25/nx-s1-5760675/iran-war-military-deployment)
- [US-Israel strikes on Iran briefing — UK Parliament](https://commonslibrary.parliament.uk/research-briefings/cbp-10521/)
